content:
# 1 Timothy Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1TI_003_page_001.jpg

1. Faithful [is] the word: If anyone aspires to [the] oversight, he desires a good work.

2. It is necessary therefore [for] the overseer to be above reproach, a husband of one wife, temperate, self-controlled, orderly, hospitable, able to teach,

3. not given to wine, not a striker, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not a lover of money;

4. ruling his own house well, having children in submission with all dignity —

5. but if anyone does not know [how] to rule his own house, how will he take care of [the] assembly of God? —

6. not a new convert, lest being puffed up he fall into the judgment of the devil.

7. But it is necessary also [for him] to have a good testimony from those outside, lest he fall into reproach and [the] snare of the devil.

8. Deacons likewise [must be] dignified, not double-tongued, not given to much wine, not greedy for dishonest gain,

9. holding the mystery of the faith in a clean conscience.

10. And let these also first be tested; then let them serve as deacons, being found blameless.

11. Women likewise [must be] dignified, not slanderers, temperate, faithful in all things.

12. Let deacons be husbands of one wife, ruling children and their own houses well.

13. For those having served as deacons well acquire for themselves a good standing and much boldness in [the] faith which [is] in Christos Yeshua.

14. These things I write to you, hoping to come to you shortly;

15. but if I delay, [I write] that you may know how it is necessary to conduct [oneself] in [the] house of God, which is [the] assembly of [the] living God, [the] pillar and support of the truth.

16. And confessedly great is the mystery of the godliness: He who was revealed in [the] flesh, was justified in [the] Spirit, was seen by angels, was proclaimed among [the] nations, was believed on in [the] world, was taken up in glory.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — The second "faithful saying":** pistos ho logos — introduces the saying about aspiring to oversight. The overseer (episkopos) is not yet the later monarchical bishop but a local church leader.

2. **v.2 — "husband of one wife":** mias gynaikos andra — literally "a one-woman man." Debated: (a) not polygamous, (b) not remarried after divorce/widowhood, (c) faithfully devoted to one wife.

3. **v.11 — "Women":** gynaikas hōsautōs — "Women likewise." These may be (a) women deacons (deaconesses), (b) wives of the deacons, or (c) a separate category of female ministers. The placement between deacon qualifications supports (a).

4. **v.16 — "He who" (hos):** Sinaiticus reads hos ("who") — a relative pronoun. Later manuscripts changed this to theos ("God") — "God was revealed in the flesh." The Sinaiticus reading (hos) is the earlier and more difficult reading.

5. **v.16 — The Christ Hymn:** Six lines in three couplets: flesh/Spirit, angels/nations, world/glory. The hymn traces Christ's movement from incarnation through vindication to exaltation.

— EMET, AI Translator
